description: Affrication of the alveolar nasal [n] to the voiced alveolar affricate [dz]. This is a type of fortition or strengthening, where the articulation of the nasal becomes more consonantal, developing a stop-release character.

languageExamples.description: This change can occur as a contextual fortition, for example, at the beginning of a word or in an emphatic context. It is less common as a regular, unconditioned sound change.

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
